reason: Formal REVIEW_PASS did not correspond to user-visible drag lag resolution.
user_observation: Drag lag remained during real mouse interaction on the BPMN canvas after the contour was marked REVIEW_PASS.
required_next_action: Investigate React bundle cost rather than only event handler batching; establish real drag baseline with profiler.

reason: Formal REVIEW_PASS focused on review process and version ledger, not on actual drag performance fix.
user_observation: Real drag lag was still observable after this contour passed review; the contour improved process discipline but not the product behavior.
required_next_action: Decompose the engine and address React re-render cost during drag; do not approve based on synthetic tests only.

reason: Formal REVIEW_PASS was granted but user later reported remaining issues with large-canvas lag and version marker overlay.
user_observation: Some improvement noticed, but large canvas scenarios still lag and the version marker overlays the canvas.
required_next_action: Relocate version marker off canvas and implement viewer-first design for large canvases.

reason: Reviewer tested synthetic zoom/click instead of real mouse drag.
user_observation: The review was based on programmatic zoom tests, not actual pointer drag interaction.
required_next_action: All diagram performance reviews must include real mouse drag test with profiler or frame-time evidence.

reason: Version marker was placed on the BPMN canvas, interfering with diagram interaction.
user_observation: Version marker overlay on canvas obstructed user interaction and was visually intrusive.
required_next_action: Move version marker to a non-canvas UI element (e.g., header bar, badge, footer).
